Barack Obama:
Biography of Barack Obama

Early Life and Education
Barack Hussein Obama (Barack Hussein Obama Jr.) was born on 4 November, 1961 in the city of Olahana of the Hawaiian island. His father, Barack Hussein Obama San (Barack Hussein Obama Sr.), was a politician of Kenya, while mother, Margaret M. Obama (Ann Dunham), was a teacher of Kansas. Obama’s father lived in Kenya, while the mother lived in America. Due to the separation of his family in his father’s childhood, Obama had very little contact with his father in his childhood. After his father, his mother raised him in America, where he was kept in his uncle’s house.

Obama obtained a bachelor’s degree in political science from Columbia University, then obtained a law degree from the law school of Harvard University. After his degree, he worked as a social worker in Chicago, where he worked for poverty and education reform.

Entry into Politics
In the 1990s, Obama contested an election as a member of a state parliament of Illinois, where he developed his political ideas. In the late 1990s, he won the election as a member of the state parliament of Illinois, where he spoke on issues of education, health and economy. In 2004, he won the election as a member of the state parliament of Illinois, after which he expanded his political ideas.

As the President of the United States
In 2008, Obama participated in the election of the President of the United States, where he expanded his political ideas. In his election, he expanded his political ideas, in which he spoke on issues of American citizenship, education, health and economy. Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping’s Biography
Birth and Early Life
Xi Jinping (Born: 15 September 1953) was born in Liaoang district of Shaanxi province, China. His father Xi Jinshang was a politician and Communist Party leader, while his mother was a teacher. Seeds of political and social awareness were sown in his environment, which are reflected in his later life and work.

Education and Political Beginning
Xi received a bachelor’s degree in chemistry from Shaanxi University, after which he began working in China’s political department. In 1969, he was posted to a rural area in a scheduled department, where he lived for two decades in rural life and gained political experience. During this period, he planted the seeds of political awareness and developed his ideas for national development.

Rising Political Leader
In the 1970s, Xi worked in higher-level jobs in China’s political department, where he gained experience in political leadership. In the 1980s, he was posted in the state Communist Party department of China, where he played an important role in national development and economic policies. In 1985, he was appointed as a senior officer in the state Communist Party department of China, where he played an important role in national development and economic policies.

Emerging National Leadership
At the end of the 1980s, Xi expanded his role for higher leadership in the state Communist Party department of China. In 1985, he was appointed as a senior officer in the state Communist Party department of China, where he played an important role in national development and economic policies. Dilma Rousseff:
Biography of Dilma Rousseff
Birth and Early Life
Dilma Vana Rousseff (Dilma Vana Rousseff) was born in 1947 in the city of Belo Horizonte of the Minas Gerais state of Brazil. Her education took place at the National Engineering Institute of Brazil (COPPE), where she obtained a degree in engineering. Her early career was spent in the public sector, where she gained experience in the field of finance and policy making.
Beginning of Political Career
In the 1980s, Rousseff joined the National Development Party (PRN) of Brazil and made her place in politics. In 1989 she was elected as the mayor of Belo Horizonte, where she expanded in the field of education, health and social facilities. After this, she won elections as a legislative member in the national parliament and later became a member of the national parliament.
Entering National Politics
In 2002, Rousseff made her place in national politics under the National Development Party (PT) of Brazil. In 2006, she was elected as a member of the national parliament of Brazil, where she played an important role in the expansion of social security programs like Bolsa Família (Bolsa Família).
First Female President
In the 2010 election, Rousseff won the election as the president of Brazil and in 2011 became the first female president of Brazil. Her administrative policies included economic solutions, social security and goals of development. However, her economic policies promoted the financial instability of Brazil during the economic crisis of 2015.
